prompt: A photorealistic aerial or elevated view of a grassy mountain slope at golden hour. Two clearly distinct worn footpaths are visible side by side, both starting from the same point at the bottom of the frame and ending at the same point at the top. The LEFT path is smooth, broad, and clean — it follows the natural slope of the mountain in one gentle efficient curve, the obvious sensible route. The RIGHT path is also clearly a worn footpath but it behaves completely differently — it zigzags and writhes erratically across the slope, weaving tightly around every individual pebble, every small rock, every grass tuft, every minor depression, obsessively tracing each tiny surface irregularity while slowly making its way up the same slope. The right path covers far more ground and looks exhausting and irrational. Both paths are equally visible, equally worn, clearly intentional. The contrast between rational simplicity and irrational obsessive detail is the entire subject of the image. Photorealistic landscape photography, wide angle, golden hour light, physically plausible. No text. No labels. No diagrams. No people.
metaphor: Two mountain paths share start and end points: one smooth trail follows the general slope; the other zigzags around every pebble and tuft, tracing noise instead of signal.
